'''Michael Oldfield''' (born 24 November 1990) is a [[Tonga national rugby league team|Tongan]] international [[rugby league]] footballer who last played as a {{rlp|wg}}er and {{rlp|ce}} for the [[Parramatta Eels]] in the [[National Rugby League|NRL]].

He previously played for the [[Penrith Panthers]], [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]], [[Canberra Raiders]], [[Sydney Roosters]] and the [[Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles]] in the [[National Rugby League]], and the [[Catalans Dragons]] in the [[Super League]].<ref>[http://www.mwse.com.au/default.aspx?s=player-profile-display&id=1081&player=michael%20-oldfield&team=Sea+Eagles/ Manly Sea Eagles Profile, Manly Sea Eagles], retrieved 25 April 2011.</ref>

==Background==
Oldfield was born in [[Manly, New South Wales]], Australia. He is of [[Tongan people|Tongan]] and [[European descent]].{{cn|date=July 2025}}

==Early years==
He attended Harbord Public School as a junior and later attended Cromer High School. For years 11 and 12, Oldfield was offered a sport scholarship to The Scots College. During his time at Scots, Oldfield predominantly played [[rugby union]].{{cn|date=July 2025}}

==Playing career==
===Club career===
====Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles====
Oldfield played 34 games for the [[Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles]] [[National Youth Competition (rugby league)|National Youth Competition]] team over the [[2009 NRL Under-20s season|2009]] and [[2010 NRL Under-20s season|2010]] seasons, scoring 23 tries.<ref>[http://www.rleague.com/db/player/o/oldfield_michael/index.php/ Michael Oldfield Stats, rleague.com] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110809132405/http://www.rleague.com/db/player/o/oldfield_michael/index.php |date=9 August 2011 }}, retrieved 25 April 2011.</ref>

Oldfield made his debut for Manly-Warringah in round 23 of the [[2010 NRL season]] against the [[St George Illawarra Dragons]].

In 2011, in each of the games he played, he scored a try. Along with fullback [[Brett Stewart (rugby league)|Brett Stewart]], Oldfield was regarded as one of the fastest runners not only at Manly but in the NRL.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.abc.net.au/news/2012-06-19/roosters-sign-michael-oldfield/4078946 |title=Oldfield to leave Manly for the Roosters |publisher=NRL |date=19 June 2012 |access-date=14 October 2021}}</ref>

Oldfield traveled with the Sea Eagles to England and played in their [[2012 World Club Challenge]] loss to the [[Leeds Rhinos]] before the start of the [[2012 NRL season]].

It was hoped that the flying winger would be the club's permanent replacement for long-time left winger [[Michael Robertson (rugby league)|Michael Robertson]], who had left the club following the [[2011 NRL Grand Final]] victory. Despite scoring 6 tries for the Sea Eagles in 2012, Oldfield struggled to cement his place in Manly's first grade team and was soon replaced by powerhouse rookie [[Jorge Taufua]]. This saw Oldfield only playing in 7 games for the 2012 season.

On 19 June 2012, it was announced that Oldfield had signed a two-year contract with the [[Sydney Roosters]] from 2013. Since the announcement, Oldfield was not selected in Manly's first grade team until called up due to an injury to [[David Williams (rugby league)|David Williams]] just before the [[2012 NRL season#Finals|2012 NRL Finals]]. Instead, finding himself in the Sea Eagles [[New South Wales Cup]] squad for most of the season.<ref>[http://www.heraldsun.com.au/sport/nrl/sydney-roosters-sign-winger-michael-oldfield-from-nrl-rival-manly-sea-eagles-on-two-year-deal/story-e6frfgbo-1226400737926 Roosters sign Oldfield from 2013]</ref>

====Sydney Roosters====
Oldfield signed with the [[Sydney Roosters]] for 2013. After making his club debut in a 22–24 loss to the [[Canberra Raiders]] at [[Canberra Stadium]] in round 5 of the [[2013 NRL season]] in which he scored a try, Oldfield found himself playing in the [[NSW Cup]] for the [[Newtown Jets]].
[[File:Michael Oldfield rugby.JPG|thumb|right|Oldfield playing for the Catalans Dragons in 2014]]

====Catalans Dragons====
In December 2013, Oldfield signed a two-year contract with the [[Catalans Dragons]].<ref>{{cite web|last=Oldfield|first=Michael|title=CATALANS DRAGONS SIGN MICHAEL OLDFIELD|url=http://www.zerotackle.com/super-league/catalan-dragons-sign-michael-oldfield-1217/|work=Zero Tackle|publisher=Catalans Dragons|accessdate=11 December 2013|url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20131211080537/http://www.zerotackle.com/super-league/catalan-dragons-sign-michael-oldfield-1217/|archivedate=11 December 2013|df=dmy-all}}</ref>

In the 2014 season, Oldfield scored 12 tries in just 17 games. On 14 June 2014 against [[St Helens R.F.C.]], he scored a hat trick during [[Catalans Dragons]]' 42–0 home win.

====South Sydney====
On 6 October 2015, it was announced that Oldfield had signed a one-year contract with the [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]].<ref>{{cite web|last=Oldfield|first=Michael|title=Rabbitohs Re-sign Goodwin & Confirm Signing Michael Oldfield|url=http://www.rabbitohs.com.au/content/rabbitohs/club/members-first/rabbitohs_re_sign_go.html?camefrom=EMCL_794315_33545897|work=South Sydney Rabbitohs|publisher=South Sydney Rabbitohs|accessdate=6 October 2015|url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304074329/http://www.rabbitohs.com.au/content/rabbitohs/club/members-first/rabbitohs_re_sign_go.html?camefrom=EMCL_794315_33545897|archivedate=4 March 2016|df=dmy-all}}</ref>

In 2016, he made his debut for South Sydney from which he scored two tries in his last three games.  Oldfield also spent time playing for Souths feeder club side the [[North Sydney Bears]] in the [[Intrust Super Premiership NSW]] making 5 appearances and scoring 5 tries.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.ssralmanac.com/mobile/pastplayers_bears.htm|title=South Sydney Rabbitohs North Sydney Bears Affiliated Players}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://websites.sportstg.com/assoc_page.cgi?client=7-6942-0-0-0&sID=141486&&news_task=DETAIL&articleID=43449892 |title=Wests Tigers outclass North Sydney Bears at Moss Vale |publisher=Greater Southern Region Country Rugby League |date=4 May 2016 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181215222511/http://websites.sportstg.com/assoc_page.cgi?client=7-6942-0-0-0&sID=141486&&news_task=DETAIL&articleID=43449892 |archive-date=15 December 2018}}</ref>

====Penrith Panthers====
Oldfield then signed to the [[Penrith Panthers]] signing a one-year deal.  
[[File:MICHAEL OLDFIELD Penrith.jpg|thumb|right|330px|Oldfield playing for the Panthers in 2017]]
In the same year, he was granted a release to join the [[Canberra Raiders]] on a two-year deal.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.zerotackle.com/raiders-sign-michael-oldfield-liam-knight-28082/ |title=Raiders sign Michael Oldfield and Liam Knight |publisher=Zero Tackle |date=29 June 2017|accessdate=19 March 2018}}</ref>

====Canberra Raiders====
Oldfield made 8 appearances for Canberra in the [[2018 NRL season]] and scored 6 tries including a [[hat-trick]] against [[North Queensland Cowboys|North Queensland]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/nrl-oldfield-hat-trick-helps-raiders-blow-cowboys-away-20180714-p4zrhv.html/|title=Oldfield hat-trick helps Raiders blow Cowboys away|website=SMH|date=14 July 2018}}</ref>

Oldfield made 9 appearances for Canberra in the [[2019 NRL season]] and scored 5 tries.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.canberratimes.com.au/story/6129265/oldfield-the-perfect-man-for-raiders-injury-crisis/|title=Michael Oldfield the 'perfect man' for Raiders' injury crisis|website=The Canberra Times|date=17 May 2019}}</ref>

Oldfield played six games for Canberra in the [[2020 NRL season]] scoring no tries as the club reached the preliminary final but were defeated by Melbourne.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://wwos.nine.com.au/nrl/ricky-stuart-storms-out-of-canberra-raiders-press-conference-after-one-question/edab3d4d-0eeb-4960-acc0-89d06797f8e0/|title=Canberra Raiders coach Ricky Stuart storms out of press conference after one question|website=wwos.nine.com.au}}</ref>

====Parramatta Eels====
In November 2020, he signed a contract to join Parramatta for the [[2021 NRL season]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2020-jack-bird-signs-with-dragons-brisbane-broncos-signings-tracker-transfer-centre-contracts/news-story/1dc60fc1454391200fa5e14ed0172ef6/|title=Eels announce trio of signings|website=www.foxsports.com.au|date=7 November 2020}}</ref>

In round 22 of the [[2021 NRL season]], he made his club debut against one of his former sides, Manly.  Oldfield played on the wing in Parramatta's 56–10 loss where he was caught out of position on several occasions leading to Manly tries.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2021-ryan-matterson-sent-off-brad-parker-shoulder-charge-sea-eagles-vs-eels-round-22/news-story/4bd3e7d333332a724c502a08eb365e01/|title=Eels star's season could be over as 'shocking' high shot earns a HUGE suspension|website=www.foxsports.com.au|date=15 August 2021}}</ref>
On 22 September 2021, Oldfield was released by the Parramatta club.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.sportingnews.com/au/league/news/nrl-2021-parramatta-eels-departing-players-blake-ferguson-rugby-league-joey-lussick/1ckdao762ec5j1s1xfshi22jb0/|title=Blake Ferguson's NRL career likely over after Parramatta Eels announce departures of four players|website=www.sportingnews.com}}</ref>

===International career===
On 20 April 2013, Oldfield, who is of [[Tongans|Tongan]] heritage from his mother, made his international debut for [[Tonga national rugby league team|Tonga]] in the Pacific Rugby League International against [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoa]] at [[Centrebet Stadium Penrith]].

Oldfield earned a second cap in 2016, featuring on the wing against rivals [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoa]] in the [[2016 Pacific Rugby League Tests|2016 Pacific Rugby League test match]] at [[Pirtek Stadium]].
